prompt: Create a cinematic 35mm editorial photograph of a young woman sitting backwards on a modern chrome tubular chair in the center of an enormous underground subway station transformed into a surreal graphic-design studio. She faces the camera with her forearms folded over the chair back, relaxed and confident, wearing a dark graphite oversized button-up shirt, loose wide-leg white pants, and chunky white slide sandals. Use a realistic face matching the chosen character, natural long wavy dark hair, soft neutral expression, and shallow depth of field. The station is industrial and moody, with wet reflective concrete floors, railway tracks receding into the background, overhead fluorescent strip lights, exposed pipes, concrete columns, and distant train headlights glowing through haze. The floor and walls are covered with creative-work clutter: exactly 2 open laptops showing design/video-editing interfaces, exactly 3 loose camera lenses, exactly 1 black camera body in the foreground, plus scattered sketch papers, posters, notebooks, prints, and presentation boards. Add large graphic-design signage and typography integrated into the environment: exactly 7 prominent readable slogan placements, including an overhead black sign reading “Design Is Everywhere” with an upward arrow, a large left wall sign reading “DESIGN IS EVERYWHERE.”, a small left label reading “CREATE THE UNEXPECTED.”, a small left label reading “STAY ORIGINAL.”, a small centered far sign reading “CREATE THE UNEXPECTED.”, a glowing orange right sign reading “STAY ORIGINAL.”, and a right wall sign reading “DESIGN IS EVERYWHERE”. Style the image like a high-end fashion campaign mixed with a design-school installation: muted teal-gray and warm amber color grade, gritty urban realism, volumetric light, glossy reflections, detailed background posters, balanced symmetrical composition, full-body seated pose, vertical 2:3 framing, ultra-realistic photography, no cartoon effects, no extra people, no watermark.
